name: Radical Atoms
short_description: A vision for transformable physical materials that are as dynamically reconfigurable as on-screen pixels and bidirectionally coupled to an underlying digital model.
long_description: Radical Atoms, proposed by Hiroshi Ishii and colleagues at the MIT Media Lab Tangible Media Group, extends the earlier Tangible Bits agenda toward a future in which the material itself becomes the interface. The vision imagines computationally transformable matter that changes its shape, appearance, and properties in real time, kept in two-way sync with a digital model so physical and digital states continuously reflect one another. As physical AI and interaction design, it reframes embodiment itself as a malleable medium rather than a fixed enclosure for computation, anticipating shape-changing and programmable-matter interfaces. It is an articulated vision and research agenda rather than a single built system.
ai_note: Radical Atoms is a vision about computationally transformable, bidirectionally coupled materials and tangible interaction; it does not posit or implement AI/ML.

images.caption: Diagram of the Tangible Bits to Radical Atoms progression and the Material User Interface concept from the Tangible Media Group vision page.
images.alt_text: A conceptual diagram illustrating the evolution from Tangible Bits to Radical Atoms, showing materials whose physical form is bidirectionally coupled with an underlying digital model.

sources.title: Radical Atoms (Vision)
sources.publisher: MIT Media Lab, Tangible Media Group

sources.title: Radical atoms: beyond tangible bits, toward transformable materials
